diagrams.title: Cayley-Hamilton Theorem Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between matrix, characteristic polynomial, and the theorem's application
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the theorem statement and verification process

diagrams.title: Power Reduction Method
diagrams.description: Visual representation of reducing high powers of matrix to lower degree polynomial
elements: End: Polynomial in A of degree ≤ n-1
diagrams.purpose: Learning to compute high powers without direct multiplication

diagrams.title: Finding Inverse via CHT
diagrams.description: Step-by-step process to compute matrix inverse using characteristic polynomial coefficients
elements: Compute characteristic polynomial coefficients c₀, c₁, ..., c_{n-1}
diagrams.purpose: Systematic method for computing matrix inverse

diagrams.title: Eigenvalue-Matrix Connection
diagrams.description: Shows how eigenvalues relate to characteristic polynomial and Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
elements: Eigenvalues λ₁, λ₂, ..., λₙ on number line
For eigenvalue λ, eigenvector v: p(A)v = p(λ)v = 0
